scope_contract: cleanliness-of-THIS-diff only; emits new-in-this-diff findings tagged via Mechanism (ii) inspector-side tagging with explicit introduced_in_diff field on every finding
out_of_scope_reason: CLAUDE.md is global configuration / standing instructions, not an agent/command execution doc. 'Rules not stories' principle applies to .claude/agents/*.md and .claude/commands/*.md; not skipped silently — explicitly excluded with audit trail.

diff_insertion_summary.note_on_largest: Corrected per codex CF-1: prior draft mis-identified commands/commit.md +43 as largest; +43 was total-changed (36 add + 7 del). Actual largest insertion is agents/qa.md +42, below the minor=50 threshold.

advisory_observations.location: Push-analyst grant TTL section + Step 5 explanatory rationale (approx lines 186-190 and 203-215)
advisory_observations.category: story_smell — rationale-heavy explanatory prose embedded in a rule
advisory_observations.description: The TTL section devotes ~10 lines to WHY 180s was chosen (subagent dispatch latency narrative, end-to-end deadline math, distinction from commit-grant TTL). 'Rules not stories' would prefer the constant declaration plus a one-line distinct-mechanism note.
advisory_observations.recommendation_per_codex_PROPOSED_FIX: Compress TTL section to: 'Set PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180 in agents/push-analyst.md. This Chain-B TTL is distinct from commit-grant GRANT_TTL_MINUTES = 10; do not change commit-grant TTL here.'
advisory_observations.reason_non_blocking: Below minor=50 file-level threshold (+37 total added). Logged as cleanup candidate for a future hygiene cycle.

advisory_observations.location: Reversal-citation rule 'Two independent rules apply here, distinct in scope' block (approx lines 250-263)
advisory_observations.category: story_smell — duplicate-rule restatement and cross-reference clarifying prose
advisory_observations.description: The block re-states the destructive-verb prohibition that already lives in agents/ba.md Destructive-Action Escalation, then recapitulates the reversal-citation rule that was just stated in the preceding paragraph. Adds ~14 lines of clarifying prose without introducing new executable content.
advisory_observations.recommendation_per_codex_PROPOSED_FIX: Replace approximate lines 250-263 with: 'This is a forward-fix documentation rule only. It does NOT authorize git revert, git reset --hard, git rebase, amend, or force-push; those remain forbidden unless explicitly user-authorized. Apply prospectively; do not amend past commits such as d988d4a.'
advisory_observations.reason_non_blocking: Below minor=50 file-level threshold (+32 added). However the duplicate-rule cross-reference is the second-strongest cleanup candidate in the cycle after commands/push.md.

advisory_observations.location: Verification harness cleanup contract — 'structural ordering is REQUIRED' paragraph (within the new MANDATORY section)
advisory_observations.category: story_smell — mechanism explanation embedded in rule
advisory_observations.description: Explains WHY the runtime double-quoted trap form is idempotent (variable re-read at fire time, etc) across ~5 lines. The canonical pattern itself is shown in the bash block immediately above; explanation could be replaced with 'use this pattern verbatim'.
advisory_observations.recommendation_per_codex_PROPOSED_FIX: Optional compression: replace the 5-line explanation with a single-line cross-reference: 'Use the pattern verbatim — the runtime double-quoted PID expansion is what makes the trap idempotent.' Mechanism explanation belongs in /dev hygiene docs, not the contract section.
advisory_observations.reason_non_blocking: Below minor=50 file-level threshold (+42 added). The contract itself plus DO-NOT clauses are substantive rules; only the mechanism explanation drifts into 'stories'.

advisory_observations.location: R3 TodoWrite-ordering reminders inserted at Step 8 (dev.md) and Step 1 (close.md)
advisory_observations.description: Each reminder restates the TodoWrite-before-Agent rule across 3 near-identical sentences ('MUST emit … BEFORE invoking', 'REQUIRED ordering: TodoWrite first, Agent() second', 'Always update the in_progress marker BEFORE dispatch', 'Before dispatch … must already be in_progress').
advisory_observations.recommendation_per_codex_PROPOSED_FIX: Reduce each reminder to one sentence: 'TodoWrite update Step-N to in_progress BEFORE any Agent() dispatch in Step N (REQUIRED ordering).'
advisory_observations.reason_non_blocking: Below minor=50 file-level threshold. The restatement is intentionally emphatic for a binding ordering rule; redundancy here is defensible vs cycle history of ordering-violation regressions.

summary.final_wording_per_codex_CF5: No blocking prompt-inspector finding. Advisory cleanup candidates exist in commands/push.md (TTL rationale ~10 lines) and agents/changelog-analyst.md (duplicate-rule block ~14 lines), but introduced verbose content remains below minor severity (file-level 50-line threshold strictly binds per codex CF-4) and does not justify CLOSE:NO.

severity_threshold_application.binding_per_codex_CF4: absolute file-level thresholds bind; aggregated multi-file insertions (165 total in this cycle) do NOT promote individual sub-threshold blocks to findings

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed the verdict (0/0/0, no CLOSE:NO) but flagged: (1) counting error in draft (commit.md +43 was total-changed, not insertion; actual largest = qa.md +42); (2) push.md TTL rationale undercounted as 3 lines, actual ~10-11 — strongest cleanup candidate; (3) changelog-analyst duplicate-rule block undercounted as ~10 lines, actual ~14; (4) severity calibration confirmed — file-level 50-line threshold strictly binds; (5) advised final wording log advisory candidates rather than say 'nothing notable'.

findings.issue: COUNTING_ERROR — draft said commands/commit.md +43 is largest insertion; actual largest insertion is agents/qa.md +42; +43 was total-changed (36 add + 7 del) for commit.md
findings.proposed_fix: Replace draft text with: 'Largest actual insertion count is agents/qa.md at +42 lines; commands/push.md is +37 and commands/commit.md is +36. All remain below the 50-line minor threshold.'
findings.rationale: Counting accuracy is fundamental to inspector credibility. Report's diff_insertion_summary now reflects per-file added counts and identifies agents/qa.md +42 as the largest insertion.

findings.issue: MISSED_ADVISORY — commands/push.md TTL rationale + Step 5 explanatory prose closer to 10-11 lines, not the draft's '3-line' characterization; strongest cleanup candidate of the cycle
findings.proposed_fix: Compress TTL section to: 'Set PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180 in agents/push-analyst.md. This Chain-B TTL is distinct from commit-grant GRANT_TTL_MINUTES = 10; do not change commit-grant TTL here.'
findings.rationale: Logged as advisory_observation for commands/push.md with verbose_lines_estimate=10 and verbatim codex PROPOSED_FIX wording recorded as recommendation_per_codex_PROPOSED_FIX. Not promoted to finding because file-level total (+37) below 50.

findings.issue: MISSED_ADVISORY — agents/changelog-analyst.md 'Two independent rules' block + prospective/frozen-commit note spans ~14 lines, not draft's ~10; story-like restatement
findings.proposed_fix: Replace approximate lines 250-263 with: 'This is a forward-fix documentation rule only. It does NOT authorize git revert, git reset --hard, git rebase, amend, or force-push; those remain forbidden unless explicitly user-authorized. Apply prospectively; do not amend past commits such as d988d4a.'
findings.rationale: Logged as advisory_observation for agents/changelog-analyst.md with verbose_lines_estimate=14 and verbatim codex PROPOSED_FIX wording. Not promoted to finding because file-level total (+32) below 50.

findings.issue: SEVERITY_CALIBRATION — confirms file-level 50-line minor threshold strictly binds; does not aggregate cross-file
findings.rationale: Confirms calibration. Aggregating 165 cycle insertions across files into a finding would violate the spec's per-file granularity. Recorded in severity_threshold_application.binding_per_codex_CF4.

findings.issue: ANTI-FRAUD — draft is not rubber-stamping the verdict but should not say 'nothing notable'; should log below-threshold advisory cleanup candidates explicitly
findings.proposed_fix: Final wording: 'No blocking prompt-inspector finding. Advisory cleanup candidates exist in commands/push.md and agents/changelog-analyst.md, but introduced verbose content remains below minor severity and does not justify CLOSE:NO.'
findings.rationale: Verbatim wording recorded in summary.final_wording_per_codex_CF5. Four advisory_observations populated to give downstream consumers a concrete cleanup backlog while keeping the formal verdict at 0/0/0.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 5 codex findings applied: counting error corrected with per-file insertion table; 2 missed advisories logged as advisory_observations with verbatim codex PROPOSED_FIX recommendations; severity calibration recorded as binding constraint; anti-fraud final wording adopted verbatim. Final verdict unchanged (0 critical / 0 major / 0 minor) — codex agreed verdict is correct but draft was 'mildly under-adversarial' on logging detail.
